summary:
Fixed the deadlock “needle”.

What was wrong:
• TransferService locked the two account objects in call-site order (from → to).  
  Two simultaneous opposite transfers (A→B and B→A) could lock A then B and B then A, producing a circular wait and deadlock.

What I changed:
• Implemented deterministic lock ordering in TransferService:
  – Decide lock order via System.identityHashCode of each account’s lock.  
  – If the hash codes collide, fall back to a global tie-breaker lock, then lock the two accounts in a stable order.
• Replaced manual int counters with AtomicInteger for thread-safe statistics.
• Moved the real debit/credit logic into a helper method to keep code clean.

Verification:
Running the supplied test script twice now completes without timeouts or balance errors:

PASS: All concurrent transfers completed without deadlock
